coredata.dc:title: Solid Electrolyte Li1.4Al0.4Ti1.6(PO4)3 as Coating for High Voltage Spinel LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4 Cathode Material
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2020 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Wu, Dan
Li, Wei
Tegus, O.
Yang, Yanchun
Tian, Xiao
Bator, Siqin
coredata.dc:description: A high voltage spinel lithium manganese oxide LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4(LNMO) was coated with solid electrolyte Li1.4Al0.4Ti1.6(PO4)3 by a sol-gel method. The experimental samples were characterized by scanning electron microscopy, transmission electron microscopy, X-ray diffraction, charge-dischargement analysis, and electrochemical impedance analysis. The experimental results show that coating improves the cyclic performance of LNMO. The capacity retention ratio of the sample coated with 0.50 wt% Li1.4Al0.4Ti1.6(PO4)3 was 98.1% and 96.1% at temperatures of 25 °C and 55 °C, respectively, after 100 cycles and a 1 C rate. Compared with the research results of a TiO2 coating agent, Li1.4Al0.4Ti1.6(PO4)3 can promote the diffusion of lithium ions on the surface of the LNMO electrode, reduce the migratory impedance of lithium ions on the surface of the electrode, and improve the rate capability. In contrast, TiO2 increases the transport resistance of the lithium ion on the electrode surface and the rate capability decreases. Under the condition of a 20 C rate, the sample coated with Li1.4Al0.4Ti1.6(PO4)3 exhibits 81.3% of its initial capacity, while the sample coated with TiO2 only exhibits 74.5% of its initial capacity.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Lithium-ion battery
Cathode material
High voltage spinel lithium manganese oxide
Surface coating
Solid electrolyte

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: rawtext articleinfo articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issfirst issn issnnorm issuelist itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight oauserlicense openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pglast pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content oa subj tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast primabst ref

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Solid Electrolyte Li1.4 Al Al0.4 Ti Ti1.6 (PO (PO4 ) )3 as Coating for High Voltage Spinel LiNi as Coating for High Voltage Spinel LiNi0.5 Mn Mn1.5 O O4 Cathode MaterialDan Wu, 1 Wei Li, 1 2 liweisyn@163.com O. Tegus, 1 2 Yanchun Yang, 1 2 Xiao Tian, 1 2 Siqin Bator, 1 2 1 College of Physics and Electronic Information, Inner Mongolia Normal University, Hohhot 010022, China. College of Physics and Electronic Information Inner Mongolia Normal University Hohhot 010022 China College of Physics and Electronic Information, Inner Mongolia Normal University, Hohhot 010022, China. 2 Inner Mongolia, Key Laboratory for Physics and Chemistry of Functional Materials, Hohhot 010022, China. Inner Mongolia Key Laboratory for Physics and Chemistry of Functional Materials Hohhot 010022 China Inner Mongolia Key Laboratory for Physics and Chemistry of Functional Materials, Hohhot 010022, China. A high voltage spinel lithium manganese oxide LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4(LNMO) was coated with solid electrolyte Li1.4 Al Al0.4 Ti Ti1.6 (PO (PO4 ) )3 by a sol-gel method. The experimental samples were characterized by scanning electron microscopy, transmission electron microscopy, X-ray diffraction, charge-dischargement analysis, and electrochemical impedance analysis. The experimental results show that coating improves the cyclic performance of LNMO. The capacity retention ratio of the sample coated with 0.50 wt% Li by a sol-gel method. The experimental samples were characterized by scanning electron microscopy, transmission electron microscopy, X-ray diffraction, charge-dischargement analysis, and electrochemical impedance analysis. The experimental results show that coating improves the cyclic performance of LNMO. The capacity retention ratio of the sample coated with 0.50 wt% Li1.4 Al Al0.4 Ti Ti1.6 (PO (PO4 ) )3 was 98.1% and 96.1% at temperatures of 25 Â°C and 55 Â°C, respectively, after 100 cycles and a 1 was 98.1% and 96.1% at temperatures of 25 Â°C and 55 Â°C, respectively, after 100 cycles and a 1 C rate. Compared with the research results of a TiO rate. Compared with the research results of a TiO2 coating agent, Li coating agent, Li1.4 Al Al0.4 Ti Ti1.6 (PO (PO4 ) )3 can promote the diffusion of lithium ions on the surface of the LNMO electrode, reduce the migratory impedance of lithium ions on the surface of the electrode, and improve the rate capability. In contrast, TiO can promote the diffusion of lithium ions on the surface of the LNMO electrode, reduce the migratory impedance of lithium ions on the surface of the electrode, and improve the rate capability. In contrast, TiO2 increases the transport resistance of the lithium ion on the electrode surface and the rate capability decreases. Under the condition of a 20 increases the transport resistance of the lithium ion on the electrode surface and the rate capability decreases. Under the condition of a 20 C rate, the sample coated with Li rate, the sample coated with Li1.4 Al Al0.4 Ti Ti1.6 (PO (PO4 ) )3 exhibits 81.3% of its initial capacity, while the sample coated with TiO exhibits 81.3% of its initial capacity, while the sample coated with TiO2 only exhibits 74.5% of its initial capacity.Keywords Lithium-ion battery Cathode material High voltage spinel lithium manganese oxide Surface coating Solid electrolyte References 1. simple-head.ce:title: Solid Electrolyte Li1.4Al0.4Ti1.6(PO4)3 as Coating for High Voltage Spinel LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4 Cathode Material

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Physics and Electronic Information, Inner Mongolia Normal University, Hohhot 010022, China.

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Physics and Electronic Information

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Physics and Electronic Information, Inner Mongolia Normal University, Hohhot 010022, China.

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Inner Mongolia, Key Laboratory for Physics and Chemistry of Functional Materials, Hohhot 010022, China.

sa:affiliation.sa:address-line: Key Laboratory for Physics and Chemistry of Functional Materials

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Inner Mongolia Key Laboratory for Physics and Chemistry of Functional Materials, Hohhot 010022, China.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A high voltage spinel lithium manganese oxide LiNi0.5Mn1.5O4(LNMO) was coated with solid electrolyte Li1.4Al0.4Ti1.6(PO4)3 by a sol-gel method. The experimental samples were characterized by scanning electron microscopy, transmission electron microscopy, X-ray diffraction, charge-dischargement analysis, and electrochemical impedance analysis. The experimental results show that coating improves the cyclic performance of LNMO. The capacity retention ratio of the sample coated with 0.50 wt% Li1.4Al0.4Ti1.6(PO4)3 was 98.1% and 96.1% at temperatures of 25 °C and 55 °C, respectively, after 100 cycles and a 1 C rate. Compared with the research results of a TiO2 coating agent, Li1.4Al0.4Ti1.6(PO4)3 can promote the diffusion of lithium ions on the surface of the LNMO electrode, reduce the migratory impedance of lithium ions on the surface of the electrode, and improve the rate capability. In contrast, TiO2 increases the transport resistance of the lithium ion on the electrode surface and the rate capability decreases. Under the condition of a 20 C rate, the sample coated with Li1.4Al0.4Ti1.6(PO4)3 exhibits 81.3% of its initial capacity, while the sample coated with TiO2 only exhibits 74.5% of its initial capacity.

ce:keyword.ce:text: High voltage spinel lithium manganese oxide

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: N. Nitta, F.X. Wu, J.T. Lee, G. Yushin, Materials Today, 18(2015) 252.
